revision.text:
[[ja:濱名惣社神明宮]] {{Expand Japanese|濱名惣社神明宮}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Hamana Sōsha-shinmeigū | native_name = 濱名惣社神明宮 | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Hamana Sōsha-shinmeigū | coordinates = {{coord|34.811444|137.552444}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu|ar|أماتيراسو|ary|أماطيراسو|az|Amaterasu|bcl|Amaterasu|be_x_old|Аматэрасу|be|Аматэрасу|bew|Amatérasu|bg|Аматерасу|ca|Amaterasu|cs|Amaterasu|da|Amaterasu|de|Amaterasu|el|Αματεράσου|en|Amaterasu|eo|Amaterasu|es|Amaterasu|et|Amaterasu|eu|Amaterasu|fa|آماتراسو|fi|Amaterasu|fr|Amaterasu|gl|Amaterasu|he|אמטראסו|hi|अमातेरसु|hr|Amaterasu|hu|Amateraszu Ómikami|hy|Ամատերասու Օմիկամի|id|Amaterasu|it|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|jaquote|アマテラス|jv|Amaterasu|ka|ამატერასუ|ko|아마테라스 오미카미|la|Amaterasu|lb|Amaterasu|lt|Amaterasu|lv|Amaterasu|min|Amaterasu|ml|അമതേരസു|mni|ꯑꯃꯇꯦꯔꯁꯨ ꯑꯣꯃꯤꯀꯃꯤ|ms|Amaterasu|nl|Amaterasu|nn|Amaterasu|no|Amaterasu|or|ଆମାତେରାଶୁ|pa|ਆਮਾਤੇਰਸ|pl|Amaterasu|pt|Amaterasu|ro|Amaterasu|ru|Аматэрасу|sco|Amaterasu|sh|Amaterasu|simple|Amaterasu Ōmikami|sk|Amaterasu Ómikami|sl|Amaterasu|sr|Аматерасу|su|Amaterasu|sv|Amaterasu|ta|அமதெரசு|th|อามาเตราซุ|tl|Amaterasu|tr|Amaterasu|uk|Аматерасу|uz|Amaterasu|vi|Amaterasu|wuu|天照大神|zh_min_nan|Amaterasu|zh_yue|天照大神|zh|天照大神|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} | website = {{Official website|https://shinmeigu.jp/}} }} {{nihongo|'''Hamana Sōsha-shinmeigū'''|濱名惣社神明宮}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tōtōmi Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|lt=Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11642130|ltq=Q11642130|qid=Q11642130|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province}} ** Series ordinal: 2 ** P155: {{ill|Tadaki Shinmeigu|lt=Tadaki Shinmeigu|qid=Q135039580|1=Tadaki Shinmeigu}} ** P156: {{ill|Q135039581|ltq=Q135039581|qid=Q135039581|1=Wihanano Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * (provincie)|pt|Província de Tōtōmi|ru|Тотоми (провинция)|simple|Tōtōmi Province|sk|Tótómi|sr|Провинција Тотоми|tr|Tōtōmi (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Тотомі|zh_min_nan|Tôtômi-kok|zh_yue|遠江國|zh|遠江國|qid=Q1048864|1=Tōtōmi Province}} * {{ill|Q1042370|ltq=Q1042370|qid=Q1042370|1=Hamana District, Shizuoka}} == coordinate location (P625) == * Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonca|trbooks|Japonca|tt|Япон теле|tyv|Япон дыл|ty|Reo Tāpōnē|udm|Япон кыл|ug|ياپونچە|uk|Японська мова|ukbooks|Японська мова|ur|جاپانی زبان|uz|Yapon tili|vec|Łéngua japoneza|vep|Japonan kel'|vi|Tiếng Nhật|viquote|Tiếng Nhật|vivoyage|Sổ tay tiếng Nhật|vo|Yapänapük|war|Hinapon|wa|Djaponès (lingaedje)|functions|Z1830|wuu|日本闲话|xal|Нипонмудн келн|xmf|იაპონური ნინა|yi|יאפאניש|yo|Èdè Japaní|za|Vah Nyiedbonj|zgh|ⵜⵓⵜⵍⴰⵢⵜ ⵜⴰⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏⵜ|zh_classical|日語|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún-gí|zh_yue|日文|zh|日语|zhbooks|日语|zhvoyage|日语会话手册|zu|IsiJapani|qid=Q5287|1=Japanese language}} == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q455602|ltq=Q455602|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181488 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917316|ltq=Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == legal form (P1454) == * {{ill|religious corporation|lt=religious corporation|ja|宗教法人|qid=Q7311343|1=religious corporation}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * はまなそうしゃしんめいぐう == postal code (P281) == * 431-1414https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1080405003622https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県浜松市浜名区三ヶ日町三ヶ日122', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '静岡県浜松市浜名区三ケ日町三ケ日', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:濱名惣社神明宮]] {{wikidata link|Q119929616}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=浜松市}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|48|41|N|137|33|8|E}}}} |名称 = Hamana Sōsha-shinmeigū |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hamamatsu|Q185125|qid=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}{{ill|lt=Hamana-ku|Q117077169|qid=Q117077169|1=Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu}}{{ill|lt=Mikkabi-chō|Q11353987|qid=Q11353987|1=Mikkabi, Shizuoka}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mikkabi|1=UNKNOWN}}122 Ōwazanyama | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 48 | 緯度秒 = 41 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 137 |経度分 = 33 | 経度秒 = 08 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Sume-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |例祭 = {{ill|lt=August 2|Q2785|qid=Q2785|1=August 2}} |別名 = Oshinmei-sama |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} '''Hamana Sōsha-shinmeigū''' (はまなそうしゃしんめいぐう) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Mikkabi-chō|Q11353987|qid=Q11353987|1=Mikkabi, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Hamana-ku|Q117077169|qid=Q117077169|1=Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Hamamatsu|Q185125|qid=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. ===History=== Its founding details are unclear, but oral tradition says that {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|qid=Q4388732|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}, daughter of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|qid=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}, carrying the {{ill|lt=Three Sacred Treasures|Q327465|qid=Q327465|1=Imperial Regalia of Japan}} by divine command of {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Sume-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, came by ship to this area guided by {{ill|lt=Ōta-no-Mikoto|Q97496337|qid=Q97496337|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto}}, after passing through {{ill|lt=Yamato|Q13417196|qid=Q13417196|1=Yamato}}, {{ill|lt=Iga|Q11380622|qid=Q11380622|1=Iga}}, {{ill|lt=Ise|Q297053|qid=Q297053|1=Ise}}, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|qid=Q864156|lt=Mino|1=Mino Province}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Owari|1=UNKNOWN}} in search of an enshrinement site, stayed for over forty days in a temporary palace, and then moved by further divine oracle to the side of the {{ill|lt=Isuzu River|Q6089522|qid=Q6089522|1=Isuzu River}} in Watarai, Ise. The honden is said to have been used as a storage building for tribute goods sent from Hamana Kanbe to {{ill|lt=Ise Jingū|Q687168|qid=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}. It is in Itakura-zukuri (Ikago-zukuri), a rare old-style form nationwide. It was deeply revered by successive {{ill|lt=shoguns|Q15903946|qid=Q15903946|1=jiangjun}} and provincial governors; in particular, shoguns of both the {{ill|lt=Toyotomi clan|Q1521658|qid=Q1521658|lt=Toyotomi|1=Toyotomi clan}} and {{ill|lt=Tokugawa clan|Q1412769|qid=Q1412769|lt=Tokugawa|1=Tokugawa clan}} are said to have set regulations and donated 42 koku of registered rice fields in reverence[https://genbu.net/data/toutoumi/sinmei_title.htm Hamana Sōsha-shinmeigū][http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4415060 Hamana Sōsha-shinmeigū - Shizuoka Shrine Office]. It is treated as a candidate shrine for Eita Shrine of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} listHisao Hoga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/kaikokuso/kaikok2.htm Genealogy of the Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko and continuation of the clan]," 'Kojuki no Bōkan,' 2016.. It was ranked {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} on August 2, {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5. On February 11, Meiji 40, it was designated for offerings of {{ill|lt=shinsen|Q11591045|qid=Q11591045|1=Shinsen}} and {{ill|lt=heihaku|Q11481885|qid=Q11481885|1=Hanpei}}. ===Enshrined deity=== *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Sume-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} According to a precinct noticeboard, the {{ill|lt=Hamana Agatanushi|Q11582624|qid=Q11582624|1=list of agatanushi}} who ruled the Mikkabi area in ancient times enshrined their ancestral deity Ōta-no-Mikoto, and when Mikkabi became Ise shrine land in {{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|qid=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=940|Q23830|qid=Q23830|1=940}}), Amaterasu Sume-Ōmikami became principal and Ōta-no-Mikoto moved to a subordinate position. ===Subsidiary shrines=== ===Sessha=== *Ōta-no-Mikoto-sha (deity - {{ill|lt=Ōta-no-Mikoto|Q97496337|qid=Q97496337|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto}}) *Amenotanakihime Shrine (deity - {{ill|lt=Ametanaki-hime-no-Kami|Q48750893|qid=Q48750893|lt=Ametanaki-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto}}) *Ame-no-Hazuchi-o Shrine (deity - {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hazuchi-o-no-Kami|Q10940642|qid=Q10940642|lt=Ame-no-Hazuchi-o-no-Mikoto|1=Takehazuchi}}) ===Massha=== *Susanoo Shrine (deity - {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}) *Tenmangū (deity - {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|qid=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}) *Hachimangū (deities - {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|lt=Hime-no-Kami|Q22070227|qid=Q22070227|1=Himegami}}, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|qid=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}) *Kifune Shrine (deity - {{ill|lt=Okami-no-Kami|Q644201|qid=Q644201|lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|1=Kuraokami}}) *Hakusan Shrine (deity - {{ill|lt=Kukuri-hime-no-Kami|Q11619891|qid=Q11619891|1=Kikurihime}}) *Yahashira Shrine (deities - eight pillars, five male and three female) *Konpira Shrine (deity - {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}}) *Kanayama Shrine (deity - {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Kanayabiko-no-Mikoto}}) ===Cultural properties=== ===Important Cultural Property (national)=== *Honden of Hamana Sōsha-shinmeigū, one building - designated {{ill|lt=April 20|Q2524|qid=Q2524|1=April 20}}, {{ill|lt=1993|Q2065|qid=Q2065|1=1993}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 5) ===Shizuoka Prefecture designated Important Cultural Property=== *Hazuchi-o Shrine building, one structure - designated {{ill|lt=November 19|Q3026|qid=Q3026|1=November 19}}, {{ill|lt=1979|Q2484|qid=Q2484|1=1979}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 54) ===References / sites=== {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Hamana Sōsha-shinmeigū]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Hamana Sōsha-shinmeigū]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Hamana Sōsha-shinmeigū]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:浜松市の神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:浜名区の建築物]] [[Category:History of Hamana Ward]]
